summary: Governance contract for evidence-based strategic insight workflows with deterministic validation boundaries.
purpose: Provide governance-grade strategic insight contracts while ensuring every approved output remains explainable, policy-bound, and trace-linked.

approval_boundary: medium for approved API-surface tuning within the existing boundary
high for experimental API expansion, data-boundary change, or external permission escalation
forbidden_actions: Product runtime execution logic in ai-governance
Network-dependent verdict generation in the deterministic path
primary_users: architects approving policy and risk decisions

start_conditions: Update service contracts and policy contracts in a single change set.
Run bash scripts/validate_all.sh, bash scripts/test_determinism.sh, and bash scripts/benchmark_gate.sh before merge.
steady_state: Preserve evidence refs and trace refs for every approved insight.
Keep external API use limited to the declared and approved surface.
failure_response: Freeze release on failed validation, moderation violations, missing evidence refs, or benchmark regressions.
Disable experimental path outputs and route back to deterministic baseline flow.
If external API path is degraded, emit governance-safe fallback notes instead of an autonomous verdict.
rollback_reference: Revert the latest service contract change set.
Re-run validation and determinism checks to confirm baseline restoration.
access_revocation: Rotate external API credentials according to incident severity.
Remove access for non-compliant operators and record revocation trace refs.

failure_modes: External API expansion introduces unreviewed capability drift.
Asynchronous batch runs hide failures and reduce explainability.
Untracked external API usage creates contractual or regional exposure.
mandatory_controls: Moderation gates before and after release paths.
Fallback to deterministic local validation when async paths fail.
Architect review for high-tier boundary changes.

primary_artifacts: Incident records required by governance incident playbook.
retention_policy: Retain evidence summaries by trace reference policy.
Retain derived strategic insights only with linked evidence and trace refs.
Retain incident records according to the governance incident playbook.

conditions: Export only validated long-lived governance diary entries.
Redact any unexpected sensitive data before export.

access_controls: RBAC for service owners and designated reviewers only.
All access and approvals must be trace-recorded.
No anonymous or unmanaged external access.

extension_baseline: Experimental APIs are recommendation-only and never on the core verdict path.
Replace placeholder MCP bootstrap only after registry onboarding and conformance artifacts.
permissions_scope: Least-privilege declared MCP capabilities and approved external API surfaces only.
Escalate to high tier for experimental API, data-boundary, or external permission expansion.

critical_metrics.notes: Every shipped change must include evidence and trace refs.
Share of recompute events ending with degraded LLM status.

error_budget: Track burn by explainability and validation misses.
Freeze non-essential changes when burn exceeds 50 percent before mid-window.

change_log.change: Collapsed thedivineparadox governance docs into a single JSON contract bundle.
Introduced thedivineparadox governance service contract and approved external API usage scope.
